name: ty-lsp
description: Python language server using ty - an extremely fast type checker by Astral

_notes.architecture: Dual-layer LSP: lspServers gives Claude Code native push-diagnostics via ty, mcpServers gives Claude the full LSP protocol via cc-lsp-now (a standalone MCP adapter). The PreToolUse hook denies the built-in LSP() tool because it's incomplete and sometimes misleading.
_notes.phase_out_plan: The MCP layer exists because Claude Code's built-in LSP tool only exposes ~9 of 20+ LSP methods and is buggy on a few of those. As Anthropic closes the gap (see claude-code#40282), this plugin will shrink back toward pure lspServers. Track the issue before assuming any workaround here is permanent.
ephemeral_in_this_file: LSP_PREFER entries for callHierarchy and willRenameFiles — ty doesn't implement these as of 2026-04. When ty ships them, delete the PREFER entries so calls land on ty (faster, in-process incremental).
basedpyright fallback — once ty covers everything we use, remove the fallback from LSP_SERVERS too.
The LSP redirect hook — remove when Claude Code's built-in LSP() tool reaches parity and reliability with the MCP tools.
_notes.version_coupling: The LSP_SERVERS/LSP_PREFER env syntax requires cc-lsp-now >= 2.1.0. Older versions used LSP_COMMAND/LSP_FALLBACK_COMMAND (still accepted by the bridge for legacy configs).
_notes.why_uvx_cc_lsp_now: The MCP server is the cc-lsp-now package published separately (https://github.com/holo-q/cc-lsp-now). uvx downloads and runs it on demand. Users who installed it as a uv tool get the same binary on PATH — either invocation works.

lspServers._note: The built-in LSP integration. Handles push diagnostics (the <new-diagnostics> reminders after edits). This path is what makes the feedback loop automatic — it's not just a tool, it's a native Claude Code capability.

mcpServers._note: The MCP bridge. ty-lsp-extended exposes the full LSP protocol as individual MCP tools (lsp_hover, lsp_rename, lsp_move_file, etc.). cc-lsp-now probes server capabilities at startup and registers only the tools at least one server supports.

env._env_note: LSP_SERVERS is a ';'-separated priority list. LSP_PREFER pre-seeds the per-method handler cache so Claude's first call to an op ty-doesn't-implement goes straight to basedpyright — no wasted ty round-trip.

hooks._note: PreToolUse matcher 'LSP' is an exact string match (built-in tool name). Does not collide with MCP tool names like mcp__plugin_ty-lsp_ty-lsp-extended__lsp_hover. The hook denies every LSP() call and returns a redirect message pointing Claude at the lsp_* MCP tools.
